handles: creating â†’ populate ULID prefix `brn_`.

creating â†’ refuse when `organization.tenant_id !== branch.tenant_id` (`BRANCH_ORGANIZATION_TENANT_MISMATCH`, 422). Belt-and-braces on top of the DB FK â€” surfaces a clearer error than a raw FK violation.
creating â†’ refuse when `region.tenant_id !== branch.tenant_id` (`BRANCH_REGION_TENANT_MISMATCH`, 422).
creating â†’ refuse when `organization.status = 'archived'` (`BRANCH_ORGANIZATION_ARCHIVED`, 409). Cannot create a branch under an archived org.
creating â†’ refuse when `region.status = 'archived'` (`BRANCH_REGION_ARCHIVED`, 409).
creating â†’ consume 1 unit of `branch_slot` entitlement (throws `BRANCH_SLOT_QUOTA_EXCEEDED`, 402). Small=3, Medium=25, Enterprise=null.
creating â†’ default `status = 'active'` when the caller left it unset.
creating â†’ default `sort_order = 100` when unset.
creating â†’ generate `slug` from `name` via HasSlug when unset; guarantee composite uniqueness via `(tenant_id, organization_id, slug)` partial unique index.
creating â†’ seed default `opening_hours` from `branch.hours.default_*` config when the caller didn't provide one (9-5 weekday defaults, weekend closed).
creating â†’ seed default `is_default = true` when this is the first Branch under `(tenant, organization)`. Otherwise leaves as false unless the caller explicitly set true.
creating (is_default=true) â†’ demote any prior `is_default = true` row for the same `(tenant_id, organization_id)` in the same transaction (invariant: exactly one default per (tenant, org)).
created â†’ invalidate `DefaultBranchResolver` cache keyed by `(tenant_id, organization_id)`.
created â†’ fire `BranchCreated`.
created (address present + tenant has `branch_geocoding`) â†’ dispatch `GeocodeAddressJob`.
saving (organization_id changed post-create) â†’ refuse with `BRANCH_ORGANIZATION_IMMUTABLE` (422). Branches don't move between orgs (see readme Â§1.3).
saving (region_id changed post-create) â†’ refuse with `BRANCH_REGION_IMMUTABLE` (422). Branches don't move between regions (financial/tax implications).
saving (tenant_id changed post-create) â†’ refuse with `BRANCH_TENANT_IMMUTABLE` (422). Branches never cross tenants (tenancy isolation).
saving (is_default flipped true) â†’ demote every other `is_default = true` row for the same `(tenant_id, organization_id)` in the same transaction; fire `BranchDefaultChanged`.
saving (is_default flipped false with no sibling is_default=true) â†’ refuse with `BRANCH_LAST_DEFAULT` (409). At least one branch per (tenant, org) must be default.
saving (status transitioning) â†’ dispatch the correct lifecycle event per transition (BranchPaused / BranchResumed / BranchClosed / BranchReopened) alongside the umbrella `BranchStatusChanged`.

saving (status â†’ closed) â†’ refuse when active downstream rows reference the branch (`BRANCH_IN_USE`, 409). The dependency check spans planned tables (`facilities`, `teams`, `sessions`, `bookings`, `memberships`) via a config-driven registry (`branch.close.dependency_checks`) so future modules opt in without touching this observer.
saving (status â†’ closed) â†’ set `closed_date = now()->toDateString()` unless the caller explicitly supplied one (past-dated closures are allowed for post-hoc admin corrections).

saving (status â†’ active from closed) â†’ refuse when a successor branch was created under the same `(tenant, organization)` after the previous `closed_date` (`BRANCH_SUCCESSOR_EXISTS`, 409). Guards against accidental resurrection.
saving (coordinates set OR changed) â†’ fire `BranchCoordinatesUpdated` with old + new values so the Wave 3 geofencing module can recompute polygon coverage.

saving (address changed) â†’ fire `BranchAddressUpdated`; when tenant has `branch_geocoding` AND `coordinates.source === 'geocoded'` AND the change touched a locality-affecting field (line1, city, subdivision, postal_code, country_code), dispatch `GeocodeAddressJob`. Manual pin drops (source='manual') stay pinned regardless.

saving (opening_hours changed) â†’ fire `BranchOpeningHoursUpdated` with the `days_touched` array; refuse writes that use multi-period days when tenant lacks `branch_multi_period_hours` entitlement (`BRANCH_MULTI_PERIOD_HOURS_UNAVAILABLE`, 402).
saving (capacity changed) â†’ fire `BranchCapacityChanged`.
saving (amenities include tags outside the platform catalogue) â†’ refuse with `BRANCH_AMENITY_UNKNOWN` (422) unless tenant has `branch_amenities: custom` (Enterprise).
updated â†’ invalidate `DefaultBranchResolver` cache when is_default touched; invalidate `OpeningHoursEvaluator` cache when opening_hours touched.
updated (any field except *_at / *_by / metadata) â†’ fire `BranchUpdated` when no more specific event fired (dedupes against the specific-field events above).

deleting â†’ refuse with `BRANCH_PUBLIC_DELETE_FORBIDDEN` (403) unless the delete is invoked from the two-person platform-support CLI path (`branch:hard-delete` command with `--platform-support` flag). Wave 2 public API never hard-deletes.
deleted (soft delete) â†’ refuse â€” Wave 2 has no soft-delete path (public API returns 405). Kept in the observer signature for future compatibility.

forceDeleted (platform-support only) â†’ fire `BranchDeleted` with the cascade-summary payload; note the cascade actually happens at the DB layer (CASCADE FK on tenant_id + RESTRICT on downstream domain FKs â€” the platform-support flow does its own dependency cleanup first).
